textPlain: I’m having a hard time contributing anything intellectually interesting. This is emotionally terrifying to me.What can be said?
"melting in 2025 brought ice loss close to record levels".. but not record levels. it has happened before. and will happen again. the sky is not falling.
I guess we can hope America starts taking climate change seriously again instead of chasing short term stock market returns

Almost every scenario of hell has happened over the past 4.5b years to our planet. A bunch of those were extinction events. If we manage to speed run 10s of millions of years of nature in 200k years humanity, then we are indeed very special.

The record loss was 2022. The ice never came back. It is devastating. Instead of ice coming back, 2025 came very close to same devastating loss.Fortunately, when climate change causes devastating food shortages I'll have the competitive advantage over climate change deniers of being orders of magnitude smarter than they are.
Yeah, the earth has been entirely without ice before aswell but that earth was not habitable for usJust because its happened before doesnt mean its fine
40% loss in volume in 25 years and it's accelerating. Don't look up I guess.
Are you aure that in those other times when it happenes it didn't end in catastrophe?
> Swiss glaciers have shrunk by a quarter since 2015, study saysAnd what about this ice loss trend?
